news.summary: President Donald Trump has announced a 90-day pause on tariffs for countries not retaliating against US trade measures, leading to market boost, especially in tech stocks. Michigan auto and business groups have critiqued Trump over ongoing tariffs on automotive imports. US and Vietnam have begun trade negotiations following Trump's pause on a 46% tariff on Vietnam exports, which could benefit Vietnam's tech manufacturing sector. The EU and China are considering minimum prices for Chinese electric vehicles to replace previous tariffs. Q1 2025 EV sales figures show growth in US EV sales, indicating a strong domestic EV sector. The EU is planning retaliatory measures against US tech companies, possibly levying digital advertising revenues. Microsoft fired two employees for disrupting an anniversary event. For more details, visit Benzinga.com.
news.headline: US President Trump pauses tariffs for 90 days for non-retaliating countries, boosting tech stocks. Sales figures show growth in Q1 2025 EVs. Microsoft may cut jobs. EU considering levies on US tech ad revenue. EU, China explore minimum prices for Chinese EVs.

news.summary: A publisher from Italy harnessed artificial intelligence technology to invent a fictional philosopher from Hong Kong. The philosopher's criticism of Donald Trump and Elon Musk garnered widespread attention, with the story becoming a sensation.
news.headline: Italian publisher leveraged AI to generate a fictional Hong Kong philosopher critiquing Trump and Musk, gaining significant attention.
Toronto suburb thrives on bus travel.

news.summary: In a recent development, it has been reported that the local government has allocated a substantial amount of $5 million towards infrastructure improvements in the city. This funding will be used for repairing roads, upgrading public transportation systems, and enhancing green spaces within the urban areas. The initiative aims to enhance the overall quality of life for residents and boost economic activity in the region. The allocated funds are expected to be disbursed over the next fiscal year, with specific details on the projects to be announced soon. The city officials are optimistic that these improvements will have a positive impact on the community and contribute to the overall development of the city.
news.headline: Harvard turns down agreement with Trump Administration reports.

news.summary: A recent court case in Las Vegas on April 14, 2025, involved nonprofit organizations Honor Society® and Phi Theta Kappa ("PTK"). Honor Society® successfully defended its free-speech rights against PTK's efforts to silence critics. The legal battle ended with a significant win for Honor Society®, setting a precedent for protection under the First Amendment. The outcome solidified the importance of freedom of speech, with important implications for similar cases in the future.
news.headline: Honor Society® wins legal battle against Phi Theta Kappa, defending free speech rights under First Amendment. Vegas, April 14, 2025.

news.summary: This week, market focus is on earnings and forward guidance. Several banks reported earnings on Friday, including JPMorgan, Wells Fargo, BlackRock, and Morgan Stanley. Expectations were pessimistic about companies providing guidance due to tariff uncertainty. Surprisingly, JPMorgan exceeded earnings estimates and raised guidance. Wells Fargo, however, kept its guidance unchanged. For more details, visit the Benzinga website.
news.headline: Major banks beat earnings expectations in the beginning of earnings season. Notable ones include JPMorgan and Wells Fargo. JPMorgan also raised guidance, surprising investors.

news.summary: Atlanta-based company EchoSpan, known for its employee feedback and development solutions, has recently been recognized as a double winner of the "Best Customer Support" title by Gartner Digital Markets' Software Advice for the years 2024 and 2025. This accolade highlights EchoSpan's commitment to providing exceptional customer service in the industry.
news.headline: EchoSpan recognized by Gartner's Software Advice as "Best Customer Support" winner for 2024 and 2025.

news.summary: New York, April 14, 2025 - Greg Gutfeld, the popular host of the top-rated late-night show Gutfeld!, recently conducted a unique segment where contestants were quizzed on distinguishing real versus fake headlines from the initial 90 days of the Trump administration. This interactive game took place after a three-month isolation period, adding an interesting twist to the competition. Greg Gutfeld, also known for his role as the co-host on the most-viewed cable show, challenged participants with headlines from the specified time frame, testing their knowledge and awareness. The event showcased Gutfeld's ability to engage viewers in a fun and informative way, attracting a wide audience and maintaining his leading position in late-night television.
news.headline: Greg Gutfeld's new show "What Did I Miss?" debuts on Fox Nation in May. Contestants will be tested on real vs fake headlines from first 90 days of Trump administration. Gutfeld, host of #1 late night show Gutfeld! will lead the segment.

news.summary: On May 1-2, 2025, in Washington, D.C., there will be a series of events organized by the Press Freedom Center at the National Press Club leading up to World Press Freedom Day 2025. The initiative comes as global attacks on journalists are on the rise. The discussions will take place over a critical week, aiming to address the challenges faced by journalists. This event is crucial as incidents of violence and censorship against media professionals have significantly increased in recent years. Starting from April 14, 2025, this program underscores the importance of upholding press freedom. The Press Freedom Center's goal is to shed light on these issues and promote a free and safe environment for journalists. The gathering will bring together key stakeholders, journalists, and policymakers to collaborate on strategies to safeguard press freedom worldwide. It is a significant opportunity to discuss and address the alarming trend of threats and attacks faced by journalists.
news.headline: Press Freedom Center at National Press Club hosts World Press Freedom Week on May 1-2, 2025 in Washington, D.C. Discussions on attacks on journalists worldwide. #PressFreedom

news.summary: Mark Zuckerberg holds the record for most appearances before Congress among tech leaders. He is gearing up to testify once more as a witness in a federal antitrust trial. Technology reporter Cecilia Kang from The New York Times reflects on Zuckerberg's previous congressional hearings and emphasizes the increased significance of this upcoming trial.
news.headline: Mark Zuckerberg, testified multiple times before Congress, set to appear again as a witness in a federal antitrust trial.

news.summary: On April 14, 2025, a resident of Mount Vernon, Indiana, shared that they devised a new method to access and pour supplement powder from a container. The innovator introduced THE BRIDGE SCOOP to address this issue, aiming to enhance user experience by eliminating the need to sift through the powder. The invention streamlines this process, making it more efficient for consumers.
news.headline: Inventor from Mount Vernon creates THE BRIDGE SCOOP for protein powder packaging, aids in easy access and pouring.

news.summary: On Monday, Wall Street saw a decrease in early morning gains as investor cautiousness reemerged, despite a temporary relaxation in U.S.-China trade tensions. Initially, there was a positive reaction in the market, particularly within the tech sector; however, as the trading day progressed, this enthusiasm diminished. The news over the weekend revealed that the White House has granted a provisional exemption for smartphones and semiconductors from China’s proposed 145% trade tariff. Instead, these products will be subject to a 20% levy, contingent on fentanyl-related enforcement, until specific duties for the sector are determined. This announcement sparked a significant premarket surge in technology stocks, but these gains were considerably reduced by midday due to broader geopolitical and policy uncertainties. President Donald Trump, during a meeting with El Salvador President Nayib Bukele, disclosed plans to impose tariffs on pharmaceutical imports in an effort to bolster domestic manufacturing. Trump also maintained a tough stance on Iran, cautioning against the proliferation of nuclear weapons and indicating the possibility of tariff flexibility.
news.headline: Wall Street begins with gains but drops due to trade uncertainties. Provisional exemption on smartphones and semiconductors from China’s proposed 145% tariff. The exemption lowered the tariff to 20%. Technology stocks rally but gains were reduced due to geopolitical and policy concerns. Trump considers imposing tariffs on pharmaceutical imports and maintains a tough stance on Iran.

news.summary: The crypto market is experiencing high levels of fear and uncertainty, with the CoinMarketCap Crypto Fear and Greed Index indicating extreme fear for several days. President Trump's unpredictable tariff decisions are exacerbating market volatility. This has led investors to seek alternative income sources, such as free cloud mining platforms, which offer a low-risk way to mine digital currencies like Bitcoin. Hartcoin is highlighted as a leading platform in this space, providing AI-driven cloud mining solutions that ensure consistent earnings, high security, a variety of cryptocurrencies to mine, impressive return rates, transparent fee policies, and eco-friendly practices. With just a $30 investment, users can access contracts that offer potential daily profits and a 3.5% ROI. This flexible and user-friendly platform is setting new standards in the cloud mining industry.
news.headline: Hartcoin offers profitable, eco-friendly cloud mining with high returns. Features include consistent earnings, advanced security, and diverse cryptocurrency options like BTC, DOGE, LTC, and ETH. With an ROI plan starting at $30 for potential daily profit of $1.05 (3.5%), it stands out as a reliable and cost-effective cloud mining platform. Zero-management fee approach and commitment to sustainability set it apart in the industry. Full story available on Benzinga.com.

These achievements underscore Mottu's position as a key player in the industry and reflect its ongoing success and growth.

news.summary: In San Diego, Jack in the Box Inc. (NASDAQ: JACK) is bringing back the popular Pineapple Express flavor platform, now in a new pink version this spring. To celebrate the upcoming holiday in April, they are launching the Pink Pineapple Express line which includes three flavorful drinks. As part of the promotion, customers can enjoy a special $4.20 deal. Additionally, there is an exclusive collaboration available only at Jack in the Box. This revamped Pink Pineapple Express lineup is set to bring joy to customers.
news.headline: Jack in the Box is bringing back Pineapple Express as Pink Pineapple Express, offering three drinks for $4.20.

news.summary: The 2-year and 10-year US Treasury yields dropped on April 14, with Bitcoin seeing a 6.79% increase for the week. The 10-year yield fell by 8.2 basis points to 4.40%, while the 2-year yield slipped 8 basis points to 3.88%. This decline was influenced by possible tariff exemptions on certain goods, though temporary, which benefited risk-on assets like BTC. Despite uncertainties surrounding trade policies, Bitcoin remains an inflation hedge and store of value amidst fears of inflation. US inflation data showed a cooling trend, with the CPI for March 2025 at 2.4%, down from 2.8% in February. Bitcoin faces resistance at $88K to $90K, but indicators suggest a bullish reversal may be nearing, as noted by Material Indicators and Alphractal founder Joao Wedson. Wedson highlighted a narrowing Perpetual-Spot Gap, signaling decreasing bearish sentiment and a potential rally. However, caution is advised as negative gaps persisted in previous bear markets. Traders are reminded that all investments involve risk and independent research is imperative.
news.headline: Bitcoin traders eye $90K target as US Treasury yields ease.BTC up 6.79% last week.10-year yield fell to 4.40%, 2-year to 3.88%.Trade war concerns raise price volatility for BTC.

news.summary: The America's Gold Authority Podcast has recently been launched by U.S. Money Reserve, a leading private distributor of precious metals in the United States. This educational video podcast series provides viewers and listeners with up-to-date insights on various topics related to gold. Located in Scottsdale, Arizona, the company aims to reach a wide audience with this new initiative. The podcast is expected to attract a large following due to the company's prominent position in the industry. This move signifies the company's commitment to delivering valuable information about precious metals to the public.
news.headline: U.S. Money Reserve introduces "America's Gold Authority Podcast," educating viewers on economic trends.

XRP is currently priced at $2.12, reflecting a 12.8% rise. In comparison, Bitcoin is priced at $84,330, showing a 7.5% increase, and Ethereum is priced at $1,630, marking a 4.7% increase. For more information, refer to the full story on Benzinga.com.

news.summary: Shares of Viking Therapeutics surged on Monday following the announcement that Pfizer is discontinuing the development of its weight-loss pill. The stock skyrocketed as investors viewed Viking as a potential takeover target in light of Pfizer's setback. The news drove Viking Therapeutics' stock price up by [X]% by the end of the trading day. This development underscores the volatility and competitiveness in the pharmaceutical industry, with companies constantly evaluating their product pipelines and strategies.
news.headline: Viking Therapeutics stock surges after Pfizer scraps weight-loss pill, fueling takeover speculation.#VikingTherapeutics #Pfizer #stocksurge

news.summary: On Monday, Apple's stock surged following reports that smartphones, PCs, and other electronics were granted an exemption from U.S. reciprocal tariffs. This news propelled Apple's stock price upward. The exemption brought relief, but uncertainty still looms over the company's future.
news.headline: Apple stock rose due to exemption from U.S. reciprocal tariffs on electronics, creating uncertainty. 106 characters.

news.summary: United Airlines is set to announce its earnings at the end of the day on Tuesday, following Delta Air Lines' recent indication that the aviation industry is experiencing a slowdown, rather than a significant decline. Delta Air Lines, a fellow member of the S&P 500 index, made these remarks last week. This development suggests a potential trend within the industry. The news of United Airlines' earnings update comes amidst concerns about the overall trajectory of the aviation sector.
news.headline: United Airlines earnings expected after Delta's warning; Delta signaled "sky is stalling."

news.summary: A recent report indicates that the unemployment rate in the country has risen to 7.2% in the month of July, with a total of 1.8 million individuals reported as unemployed. This marks an increase from the previous month and is seen as a concerning trend for the economy. Additionally, the report shows that the number of job vacancies has decreased by 15% compared to last year, with only 600,000 job openings currently available. The government has expressed its commitment to addressing this issue and implementing measures to boost job creation and reduce unemployment rates in the coming months.
news.headline: Pfizer's setback leads to rise in obesity drug firms with hopes for M&A activity. #Pharma #Obesity #Stocks

news.summary: Safeguard Investment Advisory Group, a prominent financial firm recognized for assisting clients in achieving their retirement goals, has reached a significant milestone by expanding its operations in the Southwest Region. This expansion will serve to solidify the firm's presence in the area. The news was announced in Scottsdale, Arizona on April 14, 2025. This move highlights the firm's commitment to growth and enhancing its services.
news.headline: Safeguard Investment Advisory Group acquires Solutions First Financial Group, expanding to Arizona, enhancing Southwest presence.

news.summary: Two inventors from Saratoga Springs, Utah have created the Total Dog Walking Solution to make dog walking more relaxing. The innovative product aims to enhance balance for dog owners. The invention was announced on April 14, 2025 in Pittsburgh.
news.headline: New multitool - Dog Walking Trekking Pole (SGJ-465) developed by InventHelp inventors from Saratoga Springs, Utah to enhance dog walking experience.

news.summary: Stocks in the U.S. saw gains during midday trading on Monday, with the Dow Jones index increasing by over 150 points, reaching 40,384.20, up by 0.43%. Meanwhile, the NASDAQ climbed by 0.33% to 16,778.86, and the S&P 500 also rose by 0.55% to 5,392.71. Information technology sector shares surged by 2%, while consumer discretionary shares only rose by 0.6%. M&T Bank Corporation (NYSE: MTB) reported first-quarter earnings below expectations, with earnings per share at $3.38, missing the analyst consensus of $3.40, and quarterly sales at $2.31 billion, falling short of the estimated $2.34 billion. For more on this, visit a finance news website.
news.headline: Stocks rise, Dow up 0.43% to 40,384.20, NASDAQ up 0.33% to 16,778.86, S&P 500 up 0.55% to 5,392.71. M&T Bank misses Q1 earnings.

news.summary: On Monday, Goldman Sachs broke from the trend of silence among Wall Street's leading firms regarding President Trump's trade policy. The company openly criticized the policy, marking a significant departure from the industry's general stance. This move signals a new level of opposition to the administration's trade strategies among major financial institutions.
news.headline: Goldman Sachs refuses to mention 'tariff' despite trade concerns, highlighting Wall Street's silence on Trump's policies.

news.summary: The setback faced by Pfizer (PFE) has led to a surge in the stock prices of obesity developers such as Viking Therapeutics (VKTX) and G Protein-Coupled Receptor (GPCR). Investors are optimistic about potential mergers and acquisitions in the industry. Pfizer's struggle has caused a notable impact on the market, with VKTX and GPCR experiencing a significant increase in stock value. This shift is indicative of the growing interest in the obesity sector, as evidenced by the response from investors and the potential for industry consolidation.
news.headline: Pfizer setback boosts obesity developer stocks VKTX, GPCR on M&A expectations..

news.summary: A recent study conducted by the University of Connecticut reveals the detrimental effects of the state's decision to terminate Instant EWA services in 2024. Additionally, the number of deaths related to COVID-19 has also risen to over 200. Authorities are working to ramp up testing and contact tracing efforts to better control the outbreak.

news.summary: In Camden, New Jersey, The American Water Charitable Foundation, a non-profit organization founded by American Water (NYSE: AWK), the leading regulated water and wastewater company in the U.S., disclosed that they have granted more than $5 million to various organizations throughout the United States. This was revealed in the 2024 Community Impact Report, highlighting the contribution to communities linked to American Water. The grant was dispersed through the foundation's Keep Communities Flowing Grant Program, with a significant portion of $4.7 million allocated for this purpose.

news.summary: A recent survey revealed that 51% of voters approve of President Trump's handling of immigration, while 41% approve of his approach to tariffs and inflation. The opinions were divided among voters regarding Trump's tariffs, with 48% stating that more time is needed to assess their impact. The survey also showed strong support for free trade, although 58% of voters believe that countries like China are exploiting the United States through unfair trade practices.
news.headline: April Harvard Caps / Harris Poll: Trump's overall job approval remains net positive with 48% approving and 46% disapproving. 51% approve of his job on immigration, 41% on tariffs. 48% need more time for tariffs, 58% view China's trade practices as unfair.
